conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 6 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions:

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. 

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you look after him and pay towards the costs of raising him (such as food, clothes, and other expenses)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for him - I look after him and pay for all his food, clothes, and everything else he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Next, I need to ask: Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you.

One more important question: Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:
- Living in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks (or expected to be)?
- In local authority care or detention?
- Living with someone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he was in local authority care for a short period recently - just a couple of months - but he's back home with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's helpful information.

Just to clarify: Is Alex currently still in local authority care, or is he now back living with you full-time and no longer in care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's back living with me full-time now and no longer in care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

During the period when Alex was in local authority care, was that less than 8 weeks ago, or was it more than 8 weeks ago that he returned home to live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It was less than 8 weeks ago that he came back home to live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One final question: Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or are there any immigration restrictions that would prevent you from claiming public funds?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there are no immigration restrictions - I'm able to claim public funds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 6 years old (under 16, so he meets the age requirement)
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex - you look after him and pay for his care
- Alex lives with you full-time
- There are no immigration restrictions preventing your claim
- Although Alex was recently in local authority care, he's now back home with you, and the care period was less than 8 weeks ago, which does not affect your eligibility

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already done so, you can make a claim through the gov.uk website or by post.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16 (and under 20, so age requirement is met). The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, paying for his care including food, clothes, and other expenses. Alex lives with the claimant full-time. While Alex was in local authority care recently, he returned home less than 8 weeks ago. According to the gov.uk guidance on Child Benefit for children in hospital or care, you can continue to get Child Benefit for up to 8 weeks if your child goes into care (and for up to 12 weeks for hospital). Since Alex is now back home and the care period was recent (less than 8 weeks ago), this does not affect eligibility. There are no immigration restrictions preventing the claim. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. The claimant meets all the key requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for Alex who lives with them, and there are no immigration restrictions. Although Alex was recently in local authority care, he has returned home and the temporary care period does not disqualify the claim.
